top_signals.title: Wall Street Majors Cut Oil Forecasts on Deal to Reopen Hormuz
top_signals.summary: Wall Street’s biggest banks are cutting their oil-price forecasts for the coming quarters, as optimism grows over a revival of Middle East crude output following an interim deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
